audit_items.proof_trace: ldap_pvt_conf_option (L259-279) passes its cmd and opt arguments directly to ldap_int_conf_option without null checks. The sole in-tree caller (clients/tools/common.c:866) passes cvalue=NULL when -o KEY is given without =VALUE (strchr returns NULL at L812). Inside ldap_int_conf_option, opt is immediately dereferenced in strcasecmp (L171), strtol (L187/L230/L238), LDAP_STRDUP (L211), or strcasecmp (L200) depending on attribute type, causing a null pointer dereference.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker (or misconfigured script) invoking an LDAP client tool with '-o TIMEOUT' (without '=VALUE') triggers cvalue=NULL, which is forwarded as opt=NULL into ldap_int_conf_option, crashing the process via NPD at strtol(NULL,...) in the ATTR_OPT_TV case (L230). If libldap is embedded in a server application that exposes option-setting to external input, this becomes remotely exploitable.
